author: Walt Disney Imagineering YouTube
text:
How does everything stay in a roller coaster when it goes upside down? | Imagineer That!
Rock 'n' Roller Coaster Starring The Muppets is now open at Disney's Hollywood Studios! To celebrate the occasion, Tom Morrow 2.0 is answering the classic question - how does everything stay in a roller coaster when it goes upside down? This time around, he has a little help from Lips, trumpet player for The Electric Mayhem, as long as Joe from Legal doesn't shut this episode down...

✅ What you’ll learn:
• Roller coasters keep riders and objects in place using physics principles like inertia and centripetal force, which work together to guide motion through loops and prevent things from falling out.
• Inertia makes your body want to move in a straight line, while centripetal force from the track pulls you along the curved path.
• As long as the ride maintains enough speed and rotation, everything stays secured — but if motion stops, gravity takes over.

📖 Read more on Disney Parks Blog →
https://disneyparksblog.com/disney-experiences/imagineer-that-returns-with-tom-morrow-2-0/
 
🎥 *About the Series*
Imagineer That! is back! Follow Tom Morrow 2.0, a zany mini-animatronic with insider access to Walt Disney Imagineering, as he answers your biggest questions and reveals the magic behind Disney attractions.
